results.question: Give me all the offices in usa
results.generated_sql: SELECT * FROM offices WHERE country = 'USA'
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT * FROM offices WHERE country = 'USA';

results.question: Find all employees who work in the San Francisco office
results.generated_sql:
SELECT *
FROM employees
WHERE officeCode = (
  SELECT officeCode
  FROM offices
  WHERE city = 'San Francisco'
)
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT e.* FROM employees e JOIN offices o ON e.officeCode = o.officeCode WHERE o.city = 'San Francisco';

results.question: What is the total payment amount received from all customers?
results.generated_sql: SELECT SUM(amount) AS totalPayment FROM payments
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT SUM(amount) AS totalPayment FROM payments;

results.question: all orders that are currently shipped
results.generated_sql:
SELECT * 
FROM orders
WHERE status = 'Shipped'
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT * FROM orders WHERE status = 'Shipped';

results.question: how many customers are from France
results.generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(*) AS customerCount 
FROM customers
WHERE country = 'France'
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS customerCount FROM customers WHERE country = 'France';

results.question: who's the employee with the highest employee number
results.generated_sql:
SELECT employeeNumber, firstName, lastName 
FROM employees
ORDER BY employeeNumber DESC
LIMIT 1
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT * FROM employees ORDER BY employeeNumber DESC LIMIT 1;

results.question: give me customers with a credit limit more than 100000
results.generated_sql:
SELECT * 
FROM customers
WHERE creditLimit > 100000
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT customerName, creditLimit FROM customers WHERE creditLimit > 100000;

results.question: what is the profit margin (MSRP minus buyPrice) for each product
results.generated_sql:
SELECT productCode, productName, (MSRP - buyPrice) AS profitMargin 
FROM products
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT productName, productCode, (MSRP - buyPrice) AS profitMargin FROM products;

results.question: Show all distinct productlines
results.generated_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT productLine 
FROM productlines
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT DISTINCT productLine FROM productlines;

results.question: give me number of orders for every customer
results.generated_sql:
SELECT customerNumber, COUNT(orderNumber) AS orderCount
FROM orders
GROUP BY customerNumber
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT customerNumber, COUNT(orderNumber) AS orderCount FROM orders GROUP BY customerNumber;

results.question: Find all employees and their managers
results.generated_sql:
SELECT e.firstName AS empFirstName, e.lastName AS empLastName,
           m.firstName AS mgrFirstName, m.lastName AS mgrLastName
FROM employees e
LEFT JOIN employees m ON e.reportsTo = m.employeeNumber
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT e.firstName, e.lastName, m.firstName AS managerFirstName, m.lastName AS managerLastName FROM employees e LEFT JOIN employees m ON e.reportsTo = m.employeeNumber;

results.question: total revenue from each order
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.orderNumber, SUM(od.quantityOrdered * od.priceEach) AS orderTotal
FROM orders o
JOIN orderdetails od ON o.orderNumber = od.orderNumber
GROUP BY o.orderNumber
ORDER BY orderTotal DESC
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT orderNumber, SUM(quantityOrdered * priceEach) AS totalRevenue FROM orderdetails GROUP BY orderNumber;

results.question: products that have low stock less than 500 units
results.generated_sql:
SELECT productCode, productName, quantityInStock 
FROM products
WHERE quantityInStock < 500
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT productCode, productName, quantityInStock FROM products WHERE quantityInStock < 500;

results.question: Show the customer name and their sales representative's name
results.generated_sql:
SELECT c.customerName, 
       CONCAT(e.firstName, ' ', e.lastName) AS salesRepName
FROM customers c
JOIN employees e ON c.salesRepEmployeeNumber = e.employeeNumber
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.customerName, e.firstName AS salesRepFirstName, e.lastName AS salesRepLastName FROM customers c LEFT JOIN employees e ON c.salesRepEmployeeNumber = e.employeeNumber;

results.question: give me the highest payment amount ever received
results.generated_sql:
SELECT MAX(amount) AS highestPayment
FROM payments
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT MAX(amount) AS maxPayment FROM payments;

results.question: what is our territories
results.generated_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT territory 
FROM offices
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT DISTINCT territory FROM offices;

results.question: Count the number of employees in each office
results.generated_sql:
SELECT officeCode, COUNT(*) AS employeeCount
FROM employees
GROUP BY officeCode
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT officeCode, COUNT(*) AS employeeCount FROM employees GROUP BY officeCode;

results.question: give me orders that have not been shipped yet
results.generated_sql:
SELECT orderNumber, orderDate, requiredDate, status 
FROM orders
WHERE shippedDate IS NULL
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT orderNumber, orderDate, status FROM orders WHERE shippedDate IS NULL;

results.question: average credit limit of customers by country
results.generated_sql:
SELECT c.country, AVG(c.creditLimit) AS avgCreditLimit
FROM customers c
GROUP BY c.country
ORDER BY avgCreditLimit DESC
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT country, AVG(creditLimit) AS avgCreditLimit FROM customers GROUP BY country;

results.question: Which products have an MSRP higher than 200
results.generated_sql:
SELECT productCode, productName, MSRP 
FROM products
WHERE MSRP > 200
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT productCode, productName, MSRP FROM products WHERE MSRP > 200;

results.question: give me all job titles in the company
results.generated_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT jobTitle 
FROM employees
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT DISTINCT jobTitle FROM employees;

results.question: total quantity ordered for each product
results.generated_sql:
SELECT p.productCode, p.productName, SUM(od.quantityOrdered) AS totalQuantity
FROM products p
JOIN orderdetails od ON p.productCode = od.productCode
GROUP BY p.productCode, p.productName
ORDER BY totalQuantity DESC
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT productCode, SUM(quantityOrdered) AS totalOrdered FROM orderdetails GROUP BY productCode;

results.question: customers who have placed more than 5 orders
results.generated_sql:
SELECT c.customerNumber, c.customerName, COUNT(o.orderNumber) AS orderCount
FROM customers c
JOIN orders o ON c.customerNumber = o.customerNumber
GROUP BY c.customerNumber, c.customerName
HAVING COUNT(o.orderNumber) > 5
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.customerNumber, c.customerName, COUNT(o.orderNumber) AS orderCount FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customerNumber = o.customerNumber GROUP BY c.customerNumber, c.customerName HAVING COUNT(o.orderNumber) > 5;

results.question: Show the email addresses of all sales represintatives
results.generated_sql:
SELECT e.email
FROM employees e
WHERE e.employeeNumber IN (
  SELECT c.salesRepEmployeeNumber
  FROM customers c
)
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT firstName, lastName, email FROM employees WHERE jobTitle = 'Sales Rep';

results.question: the minimum buy price among all products
results.generated_sql:
SELECT MIN(buyPrice) AS minBuyPrice
FROM products
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT MIN(buyPrice) AS minPrice FROM products;

results.question: Find orders with comments containing the word late
results.generated_sql:
SELECT orderNumber, orderDate, comments 
FROM orders
WHERE comments LIKE '%late%'
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT orderNumber, comments FROM orders WHERE comments LIKE '%late%';

results.question: give me products sorted by quantity in stock from high to low
results.generated_sql:
SELECT productCode, productName, quantityInStock
FROM products
ORDER BY quantityInStock DESC
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT productCode, productName, quantityInStock FROM products ORDER BY quantityInStock DESC;

results.question: number of products in each product line
results.generated_sql:
SELECT productLine, COUNT(*) AS productCount
FROM products
GROUP BY productLine
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT productLine, COUNT(*) AS productCount FROM products GROUP BY productLine;

results.question: show me the full name and phone number of all customers
results.generated_sql:
SELECT customerName, phone 
FROM customers
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT customerName, contactFirstName, contactLastName, phone FROM customers;

results.question: products who is name starts with '1969'
results.generated_sql:
SELECT * 
FROM products
WHERE productName LIKE '1969%'
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT productCode, productName FROM products WHERE productName LIKE '1969%';

results.question: show me orders  with thier customer names
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.orderNumber, o.orderDate, o.status, c.customerName
FROM orders o
JOIN customers c ON o.customerNumber = c.customerNumber
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT o.orderNumber, o.orderDate, o.status, c.customerName FROM orders o JOIN customers c ON o.customerNumber = c.customerNumber;

results.question: Show me total payment  for each customer
results.generated_sql:
SELECT customerNumber, SUM(amount) AS totalPayment
FROM payments
GROUP BY customerNumber
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT customerNumber, SUM(amount) AS totalPayment FROM payments GROUP BY customerNumber;

results.question: customers without a sales representative assigned
results.generated_sql:
SELECT c.customerNumber, c.customerName
FROM customers c
WHERE c.salesRepEmployeeNumber IS NULL
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT customerNumber, customerName FROM customers WHERE salesRepEmployeeNumber IS NULL;

results.question: top 5 customers by total payment
results.generated_sql:
SELECT c.customerNumber, c.customerName, SUM(p.amount) AS totalPayment
FROM customers c
JOIN payments p ON c.customerNumber = p.customerNumber
GROUP BY c.customerNumber, c.customerName
ORDER BY totalPayment DESC
LIMIT 5
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.customerNumber, c.customerName, SUM(p.amount) AS totalPayment FROM customers c JOIN payments p ON c.customerNumber = p.customerNumber GROUP BY c.customerNumber, c.customerName ORDER BY totalPayment DESC LIMIT 5;

results.question: show me products with their product line descriptions
results.generated_sql:
SELECT p.productCode, p.productName, pl.textDescription
FROM products p
JOIN productlines pl ON p.productLine = pl.productLine
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT p.productCode, p.productName, pl.productLine, pl.textDescription FROM products p JOIN productlines pl ON p.productLine = pl.productLine;

results.question: Show all distinct countries where we have customers
results.generated_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT country 
FROM customers
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT DISTINCT country FROM customers ORDER BY country;

results.question: Find the number of customers each sales representative handles
results.generated_sql:
SELECT e.firstName, e.lastName, COUNT(c.customerNumber) AS customerCount
FROM employees e
JOIN customers c ON e.employeeNumber = c.salesRepEmployeeNumber
GROUP BY e.employeeNumber, e.firstName, e.lastName
ORDER BY customerCount DESC
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT salesRepEmployeeNumber, COUNT(*) AS customerCount FROM customers WHERE salesRepEmployeeNumber IS NOT NULL GROUP BY salesRepEmployeeNumber;

results.question: Get the total inventory value  for all products
results.generated_sql:
SELECT SUM(quantityInStock * buyPrice) AS totalInventoryValue
FROM products
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT SUM(quantityInStock * buyPrice) AS totalInventoryValue FROM products;

results.question: orders that still on hold or canceled
results.generated_sql:
SELECT orderNumber, orderDate, status 
FROM orders
WHERE status IN ('On Hold', 'Cancelled')
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT orderNumber, orderDate, status, customerNumber FROM orders WHERE status IN ('Cancelled', 'On Hold');

results.question: total revenue by product line
results.generated_sql:
SELECT p.productLine, SUM(od.quantityOrdered * od.priceEach) AS totalRevenue
FROM products p
JOIN orderdetails od ON p.productCode = od.productCode
GROUP BY p.productLine
ORDER BY totalRevenue DESC
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT p.productLine, SUM(od.quantityOrdered * od.priceEach) AS totalRevenue FROM orderdetails od JOIN products p ON od.productCode = p.productCode GROUP BY p.productLine;

results.question: average order value across all orders
results.generated_sql:
SELECT AVG(orderTotal) AS avgOrderValue
FROM (
    SELECT orderNumber, SUM(quantityOrdered * priceEach) AS orderTotal
    FROM orderdetails
    GROUP BY orderNumber
) AS orderTotals
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT AVG(orderTotal) AS avgOrderValue FROM (SELECT orderNumber, SUM(quantityOrdered * priceEach) AS orderTotal FROM orderdetails GROUP BY orderNumber) AS orderTotals;

results.question: Get customers located in California
results.generated_sql:
SELECT * 
FROM customers
WHERE state = 'CA'
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT customerNumber, customerName, city FROM customers WHERE state = 'CA';

results.question: Show the number of orders by status
results.generated_sql:
SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS orderCount
FROM orders
GROUP BY status
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS orderCount FROM orders GROUP BY status;

results.question: Find products with a scale of 1:18
results.generated_sql:
SELECT * 
FROM products
WHERE productScale = '1:18'
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT productCode, productName, productScale FROM products WHERE productScale = '1:18';

results.question: all offices with their phone numbers and cities
results.generated_sql:
SELECT officeCode, city, phone 
FROM offices
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT officeCode, city, phone FROM offices;

results.question: show me the details of the most expensive order by total value
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.orderNumber, o.orderDate, o.status, SUM(od.quantityOrdered * od.priceEach) AS orderTotal
FROM orders o
JOIN orderdetails od ON o.orderNumber = od.orderNumber
GROUP BY o.orderNumber, o.orderDate, o.status
ORDER BY orderTotal DESC
LIMIT 1
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT o.orderNumber, o.orderDate, o.customerNumber, SUM(od.quantityOrdered * od.priceEach) AS totalValue FROM orders o JOIN orderdetails od ON o.orderNumber = od.orderNumber GROUP BY o.orderNumber, o.orderDate, o.customerNumber ORDER BY totalValue DESC LIMIT 1;

results.question: Find employees who didn't report to anyone
results.generated_sql:
SELECT employeeNumber, firstName, lastName 
FROM employees
WHERE reportsTo IS NULL
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT employeeNumber, firstName, lastName, jobTitle FROM employees WHERE reportsTo IS NULL;

results.question: Show all distinct product scales available
results.generated_sql:
SELECT DISTINCT productScale 
FROM products
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT DISTINCT productScale FROM products;

results.question: how many products each vendor supplies
results.generated_sql:
SELECT productVendor, COUNT(*) AS productCount
FROM products
GROUP BY productVendor
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT productVendor, COUNT(*) AS productCount FROM products GROUP BY productVendor ORDER BY productCount DESC;

results.question: customers who have never placed an order
results.generated_sql:
SELECT c.customerNumber, c.customerName
FROM customers c
LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customerNumber = o.customerNumber
WHERE o.orderNumber IS NULL
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.customerNumber, c.customerName FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customerNumber = o.customerNumber WHERE o.orderNumber IS NULL;

results.question: Get the concatenated full name of all employees
results.generated_sql:
SELECT employeeNumber, CONCAT(firstName, ' ', lastName) AS fullName 
FROM employees
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT CONCAT(firstName, ' ', lastName) AS fullName FROM employees;

results.question: products where the MSRP is at least double the buy price
results.generated_sql:
SELECT productCode, productName, MSRP, buyPrice 
FROM products
WHERE MSRP >= 2 * buyPrice
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT productCode, productName, buyPrice, MSRP FROM products WHERE MSRP >= 2 * buyPrice;

results.question: Show me orders shipped after the required date
results.generated_sql:
SELECT orderNumber, orderDate, requiredDate, shippedDate
FROM orders
WHERE shippedDate > requiredDate
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT orderNumber, requiredDate, shippedDate FROM orders WHERE shippedDate > requiredDate;

results.question: the total number of employees in the company
results.generated_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS totalEmployees FROM employees
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(*) AS totalEmployees FROM employees;

results.question: products that have been ordered more than 1000 times in total
results.generated_sql:
SELECT p.productCode, p.productName, SUM(od.quantityOrdered) AS totalOrdered
FROM products p
JOIN orderdetails od ON p.productCode = od.productCode
GROUP BY p.productCode, p.productName
HAVING SUM(od.quantityOrdered) > 1000
ORDER BY totalOrdered DESC
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT p.productCode, p.productName, SUM(od.quantityOrdered) AS totalOrdered FROM products p JOIN orderdetails od ON p.productCode = od.productCode GROUP BY p.productCode, p.productName HAVING SUM(od.quantityOrdered) > 1000;

results.question: the average payment amount per customer
results.generated_sql:
SELECT AVG(amount) AS avgPaymentAmount
FROM payments
GROUP BY customerNumber
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT customerNumber, AVG(amount) AS avgPayment FROM payments GROUP BY customerNumber;

results.question: Show me all employees working in offices outside the USA
results.generated_sql:
SELECT e.*
FROM employees e
JOIN offices o ON e.officeCode = o.officeCode
WHERE o.country <> 'USA'
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT e.employeeNumber, e.firstName, e.lastName, o.city, o.country FROM employees e JOIN offices o ON e.officeCode = o.officeCode WHERE o.country != 'USA';

results.question: what are the top 10 best-selling products by quantity ordered
results.generated_sql:
SELECT p.productCode, p.productName, SUM(od.quantityOrdered) AS totalQuantity
FROM products p
JOIN orderdetails od ON p.productCode = od.productCode
GROUP BY p.productCode, p.productName
ORDER BY totalQuantity DESC
LIMIT 10
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT p.productCode, p.productName, SUM(od.quantityOrdered) AS totalQuantity FROM products p JOIN orderdetails od ON p.productCode = od.productCode GROUP BY p.productCode, p.productName ORDER BY totalQuantity DESC LIMIT 10;

results.question: all product lines with the number of products and average MSRP
results.generated_sql:
SELECT p.productLine, COUNT(*) AS productCount, AVG(p.MSRP) AS avgMSRP
FROM products p
GROUP BY p.productLine
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT productLine, COUNT(*) AS productCount, AVG(MSRP) AS avgMSRP FROM products GROUP BY productLine;

results.question: i wanna see the customers whose name contains 'Auto'
results.generated_sql:
SELECT * 
FROM customers
WHERE customerName LIKE '%Auto%'
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT customerNumber, customerName FROM customers WHERE customerName LIKE '%Auto%';

results.question: what's the number of line items in each order
results.generated_sql:
SELECT orderNumber, COUNT(*) AS lineItemCount
FROM orderdetails
GROUP BY orderNumber
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT orderNumber, COUNT(*) AS lineItemCount FROM orderdetails GROUP BY orderNumber;

results.question: list customers who have made payments over 50000
results.generated_sql:
SELECT c.customerNumber, c.customerName
FROM customers c
JOIN payments p ON c.customerNumber = p.customerNumber
WHERE p.amount > 50000
GROUP BY c.customerNumber, c.customerName
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT DISTINCT c.customerNumber, c.customerName FROM customers c JOIN payments p ON c.customerNumber = p.customerNumber WHERE p.amount > 50000;

results.question: the total number of orders per year
results.generated_sql:
SELECT YEAR(orderDate) AS orderYear, COUNT(*) AS orderCount
FROM orders
GROUP BY YEAR(orderDate)
ORDER BY orderYear
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT YEAR(orderDate) AS orderYear, COUNT(*) AS orderCount FROM orders GROUP BY YEAR(orderDate) ORDER BY orderYear;

results.question: show employees and their office city and country
results.generated_sql:
SELECT e.firstName, e.lastName, o.city, o.country
FROM employees e
JOIN offices o ON e.officeCode = o.officeCode
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT e.employeeNumber, e.firstName, e.lastName, o.city, o.country FROM employees e JOIN offices o ON e.officeCode = o.officeCode;

results.question: Show products that have never been ordered
results.generated_sql:
SELECT p.productCode, p.productName
FROM products p
LEFT JOIN orderdetails od ON p.productCode = od.productCode
WHERE od.productCode IS NULL
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT p.productCode, p.productName FROM products p LEFT JOIN orderdetails od ON p.productCode = od.productCode WHERE od.productCode IS NULL;

results.question: what's the totalof direct reports for each manager
results.generated_sql:
SELECT e.reportsTo, COUNT(*) AS directReports
FROM employees e
WHERE e.reportsTo IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY e.reportsTo
ORDER BY directReports DESC
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT reportsTo AS managerNumber, COUNT(*) AS directReports FROM employees WHERE reportsTo IS NOT NULL GROUP BY reportsTo;

results.question: the customers where credit limit between 50000 and 100000
results.generated_sql:
SELECT * 
FROM customers
WHERE creditLimit BETWEEN 50000 AND 100000
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT customerNumber, customerName, creditLimit FROM customers WHERE creditLimit BETWEEN 50000 AND 100000;

results.question: giv me  markup percentage for each product
results.generated_sql:
SELECT productCode, productName, ROUND(((MSRP - buyPrice) / buyPrice) * 100, 2) AS markupPct
FROM products
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT productCode, productName, ROUND(((MSRP - buyPrice) / buyPrice) * 100, 2) AS markupPercentage FROM products;

results.question: List all orders in the year 2004
results.generated_sql:
SELECT *
FROM orders
WHERE YEAR(orderDate) = 2004
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT orderNumber, orderDate, customerNumber FROM orders WHERE YEAR(orderDate) = 2004;

results.question: Get the total revenue generated by each customer
results.generated_sql:
SELECT c.customerNumber, c.customerName, SUM(od.quantityOrdered * od.priceEach) AS totalRevenue
FROM customers c
JOIN orders o ON c.customerNumber = o.customerNumber
JOIN orderdetails od ON o.orderNumber = od.orderNumber
GROUP BY c.customerNumber, c.customerName
ORDER BY totalRevenue DESC
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT o.customerNumber, c.customerName, SUM(od.quantityOrdered * od.priceEach) AS totalRevenue FROM orders o JOIN orderdetails od ON o.orderNumber = od.orderNumber JOIN customers c ON o.customerNumber = c.customerNumber GROUP BY o.customerNumber, c.customerName ORDER BY totalRevenue DESC;

results.question: give me vendors that have over 5 products
results.generated_sql:
SELECT productVendor, COUNT(*) AS productCount
FROM products
GROUP BY productVendor
HAVING COUNT(*) > 5
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT productVendor, COUNT(*) AS productCount FROM products GROUP BY productVendor HAVING COUNT(*) > 5;

results.question: whats the number of customers by city
results.generated_sql:
SELECT city, COUNT(*) AS customerCount
FROM customers
GROUP BY city
ORDER BY customerCount DESC
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT city, COUNT(*) AS customerCount FROM customers GROUP BY city ORDER BY customerCount DESC;

results.question: Get the average MSRP by product scale
results.generated_sql:
SELECT productScale, AVG(MSRP) AS avgMSRP
FROM products
GROUP BY productScale
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT productScale, AVG(MSRP) AS avgMSRP FROM products GROUP BY productScale;

results.question: Find orders where the order date is the same as the required date
results.generated_sql:
SELECT orderNumber, orderDate, requiredDate
FROM orders
WHERE orderDate = requiredDate
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT orderNumber, orderDate, requiredDate FROM orders WHERE orderDate = requiredDate;

results.question: what is the total stock quantity by product line
results.generated_sql:
SELECT p.productLine, SUM(p.quantityInStock) AS totalStockQuantity
FROM products p
GROUP BY p.productLine
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT productLine, SUM(quantityInStock) AS totalStock FROM products GROUP BY productLine;

results.question: Show payments made in 2005
results.generated_sql:
SELECT checkNumber, customerNumber, paymentDate, amount
FROM payments
WHERE YEAR(paymentDate) = 2005
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT checkNumber, customerNumber, paymentDate, amount FROM payments WHERE YEAR(paymentDate) = 2005;

results.question: Get the total revenue and quantity for each product
results.generated_sql:
SELECT p.productCode, p.productName, SUM(od.quantityOrdered) AS totalQuantity, SUM(od.quantityOrdered * od.priceEach) AS totalRevenue
FROM products p
JOIN orderdetails od ON p.productCode = od.productCode
GROUP BY p.productCode, p.productName
ORDER BY totalRevenue DESC
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT productCode, SUM(quantityOrdered) AS totalQuantity, SUM(quantityOrdered * priceEach) AS totalRevenue FROM orderdetails GROUP BY productCode;

results.question: Find customers in the same city as an office
results.generated_sql:
SELECT c.customerNumber, c.customerName, c.city
FROM customers c
WHERE c.city IN (
  SELECT o.city
  FROM offices o
)
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.customerNumber, c.customerName, c.city FROM customers c WHERE c.city IN (SELECT city FROM offices);

results.question: what arethe 5 most recent orders
results.generated_sql:
SELECT orderNumber, orderDate 
FROM orders
ORDER BY orderDate DESC
LIMIT 5
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT orderNumber, orderDate, customerNumber, status FROM orders ORDER BY orderDate DESC LIMIT 5;

results.question: what is the difference between required date and shipped date for each order
results.generated_sql:
SELECT 
    orderNumber,
    requiredDate,
    shippedDate,
    DATEDIFF(shippedDate, requiredDate) AS daysDifference
FROM orders
WHERE shippedDate IS NOT NULL
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT orderNumber, requiredDate, shippedDate, DATEDIFF(shippedDate, requiredDate) AS daysDifference FROM orders WHERE shippedDate IS NOT NULL;

results.question: what is the number of payments per month
results.generated_sql:
SELECT YEAR(paymentDate) AS year, MONTH(paymentDate) AS month, COUNT(*) AS paymentCount
FROM payments
GROUP BY YEAR(paymentDate), MONTH(paymentDate)
ORDER BY year, month
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT YEAR(paymentDate) AS year, MONTH(paymentDate) AS month, COUNT(*) AS paymentCount FROM payments GROUP BY YEAR(paymentDate), MONTH(paymentDate) ORDER BY year, month;

results.question: products with the maximum quantity in stock in each product line
results.generated_sql:
SELECT p.productCode, p.productName, p.productLine, p.quantityInStock
FROM products p
WHERE p.quantityInStock = (
    SELECT MAX(quantityInStock) 
    FROM products
    WHERE productLine = p.productLine
)
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT p.productLine, p.productCode, p.productName, p.quantityInStock FROM products p WHERE p.quantityInStock = (SELECT MAX(quantityInStock) FROM products WHERE productLine = p.productLine);

results.question: give me all customers from Germany or France
results.generated_sql:
SELECT * 
FROM customers
WHERE country = 'Germany' OR country = 'France'
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT customerNumber, customerName, country FROM customers WHERE country IN ('Germany', 'France');

results.question: total sales by sales representative
results.generated_sql:
SELECT e.firstName, e.lastName, SUM(od.quantityOrdered * od.priceEach) AS totalSales
FROM employees e
JOIN customers c ON e.employeeNumber = c.salesRepEmployeeNumber
JOIN orders o ON c.customerNumber = o.customerNumber
JOIN orderdetails od ON o.orderNumber = od.orderNumber
GROUP BY e.employeeNumber, e.firstName, e.lastName
ORDER BY totalSales DESC
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT e.employeeNumber, e.firstName, e.lastName, SUM(od.quantityOrdered * od.priceEach) AS totalSales FROM employees e JOIN customers c ON e.employeeNumber = c.salesRepEmployeeNumber JOIN orders o ON c.customerNumber = o.customerNumber JOIN orderdetails od ON o.orderNumber = od.orderNumber GROUP BY e.employeeNumber, e.firstName, e.lastName ORDER BY totalSales DESC;

results.question: i wanna know the products in alphabetical order
results.generated_sql:
SELECT productCode, productName 
FROM products
ORDER BY productName ASC
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT productCode, productName, productLine FROM products ORDER BY productName ASC;

results.question: the number of unique products sold
results.generated_sql:
SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT productCode) AS uniqueProducts
FROM orderdetails
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT productCode) AS uniqueProductsSold FROM orderdetails;

results.question: what's the first and last payment dates
results.generated_sql:
SELECT MIN(paymentDate) AS firstPayment, MAX(paymentDate) AS lastPayment
FROM payments
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT MIN(paymentDate) AS firstPayment, MAX(paymentDate) AS lastPayment FROM payments;

results.question: the orders that has more than 10 line items
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.orderNumber, COUNT(od.orderLineNumber) AS lineItemCount
FROM orders o
JOIN orderdetails od ON o.orderNumber = od.orderNumber
GROUP BY o.orderNumber
HAVING COUNT(od.orderLineNumber) > 10
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT orderNumber, COUNT(*) AS lineItemCount FROM orderdetails GROUP BY orderNumber HAVING COUNT(*) > 10;

results.question: Get the contact information for all customers in New York
results.generated_sql:
SELECT * 
FROM customers
WHERE city = 'New York'
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT customerName, contactFirstName, contactLastName, phone, addressLine1, city FROM customers WHERE city = 'New York';

results.question: the product that has  the highest MSRP
results.generated_sql:
SELECT productCode, productName, MSRP
FROM products
ORDER BY MSRP DESC
LIMIT 1
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT productCode, productName, MSRP FROM products ORDER BY MSRP DESC LIMIT 1;

results.question: give me the employees their last name starts with 'P'
results.generated_sql:
SELECT employeeNumber, firstName, lastName 
FROM employees
WHERE lastName LIKE 'P%'
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT employeeNumber, firstName, lastName, email FROM employees WHERE lastName LIKE 'P%';

results.question: the revenue trend overtime
results.generated_sql:
SELECT YEAR(o.orderDate) AS year, MONTH(o.orderDate) AS month, SUM(od.quantityOrdered * od.priceEach) AS totalRevenue
FROM orders o
JOIN orderdetails od ON o.orderNumber = od.orderNumber
GROUP BY YEAR(o.orderDate), MONTH(o.orderDate)
ORDER BY year, month
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT YEAR(o.orderDate) AS year, MONTH(o.orderDate) AS month, SUM(od.quantityOrdered * od.priceEach) AS monthlyRevenue FROM orders o JOIN orderdetails od ON o.orderNumber = od.orderNumber GROUP BY YEAR(o.orderDate), MONTH(o.orderDate) ORDER BY year, month;

results.question: orders with total valu more than 10000
results.generated_sql:
SELECT o.orderNumber, SUM(od.quantityOrdered * od.priceEach) AS orderTotal
FROM orders o
JOIN orderdetails od ON o.orderNumber = od.orderNumber
GROUP BY o.orderNumber
HAVING SUM(od.quantityOrdered * od.priceEach) > 10000
ORDER BY orderTotal DESC
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT orderNumber, SUM(quantityOrdered * priceEach) AS totalValue FROM orderdetails GROUP BY orderNumber HAVING SUM(quantityOrdered * priceEach) > 10000;

results.question: which offices in the EMEA
results.generated_sql:
SELECT officeCode, city, country 
FROM offices
WHERE country IN ('France', 'UK', 'Germany', 'Italy', 'Spain')
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT officeCode, city, country FROM offices WHERE territory = 'EMEA';

results.question: all products in the Classic Cars category
results.generated_sql:
SELECT * 
FROM products
WHERE productLine = 'Classic Cars'
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT productCode, productName, buyPrice, MSRP FROM products WHERE productLine = 'Classic Cars';

results.question: groupe customers into tier based on thier credit limit
results.generated_sql:
SELECT 
  CASE 
    WHEN creditLimit < 10000 THEN 'Tier 1'
    WHEN creditLimit >= 10000 AND creditLimit < 50000 THEN 'Tier 2' 
    WHEN creditLimit >= 50000 THEN 'Tier 3'
  END AS customerTier,
  COUNT(*) AS customerCount
FROM customers
GROUP BY customerTier
ORDER BY customerTier
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT customerNumber, customerName, creditLimit, CASE WHEN creditLimit < 25000 THEN 'Low' WHEN creditLimit BETWEEN 25000 AND 75000 THEN 'Medium' WHEN creditLimit > 75000 THEN 'High' END AS creditTier FROM customers;

results.question: how many orders did each customer make and their name
results.generated_sql:
SELECT c.customerName, COUNT(o.orderNumber) AS orderCount
FROM customers c
JOIN orders o ON c.customerNumber = o.customerNumber
GROUP BY c.customerName
ORDER BY orderCount DESC
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.customerNumber, c.customerName, COUNT(o.orderNumber) AS orderCount FROM customers c LEFT JOIN orders o ON c.customerNumber = o.customerNumber GROUP BY c.customerNumber, c.customerName;

results.question: whats the avrage quantity ordered per order line
results.generated_sql:
SELECT AVG(quantityOrdered) AS avgQuantityPerOrderLine
FROM orderdetails
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT AVG(quantityOrdered) AS avgQuantityPerLine FROM orderdetails;

results.question: all the employees who are Sales Managers
results.generated_sql: SELECT * FROM employees WHERE jobTitle = 'Sales Manager'
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT employeeNumber, firstName, lastName, email, officeCode FROM employees WHERE jobTitle LIKE '%Sales Manager%';

results.question: product code, name, and price for products priced above the average
results.generated_sql:
SELECT productCode, productName, MSRP
FROM products
WHERE MSRP > (SELECT AVG(MSRP) FROM products)
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT productCode, productName, buyPrice FROM products WHERE buyPrice > (SELECT AVG(buyPrice) FROM products);

results.question: tell me total credit limit for all customers
results.generated_sql:
SELECT SUM(creditLimit) AS totalCreditLimit
FROM customers
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT SUM(creditLimit) AS totalCreditLimit FROM customers;

results.question: which orders are still in process
results.generated_sql:
SELECT orderNumber, orderDate, status 
FROM orders
WHERE status = 'In Process'
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT orderNumber, orderDate, customerNumber FROM orders WHERE status = 'In Process';

results.question: how much each country made
results.generated_sql:
SELECT c.country, SUM(od.quantityOrdered * od.priceEach) AS totalRevenue
FROM customers c
JOIN orders o ON c.customerNumber = o.customerNumber
JOIN orderdetails od ON o.orderNumber = od.orderNumber
GROUP BY c.country
ORDER BY totalRevenue DESC
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.country, SUM(od.quantityOrdered * od.priceEach) AS totalRevenue FROM customers c JOIN orders o ON c.customerNumber = o.customerNumber JOIN orderdetails od ON o.orderNumber = od.orderNumber GROUP BY c.country ORDER BY totalRevenue DESC;

results.question: how many offices are there in each country
results.generated_sql:
SELECT country, COUNT(*) AS officeCount
FROM offices
GROUP BY country
ORDER BY officeCount DESC
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT country, COUNT(*) AS officeCount FROM offices GROUP BY country;

results.question: i wanna see the levels of employees that report to a manager
results.generated_sql:
SELECT e.firstName AS employeeFirstName, e.lastName AS employeeLastName, 
       m.firstName AS managerFirstName, m.lastName AS managerLastName
FROM employees e
LEFT JOIN employees m ON e.reportsTo = m.employeeNumber
ORDER BY m.firstName, m.lastName, e.firstName, e.lastName
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT e.employeeNumber, CONCAT(e.firstName, ' ', e.lastName) AS employeeName, e.jobTitle, m.employeeNumber AS managerNumber, CONCAT(m.firstName, ' ', m.lastName) AS managerName FROM employees e JOIN employees m ON e.reportsTo = m.employeeNumber;

results.question: customers with over 3 paymets
results.generated_sql:
SELECT c.customerNumber, c.customerName
FROM customers c
JOIN (
  SELECT customerNumber, COUNT(*) AS paymentCount
  FROM payments
  GROUP BY customerNumber
  HAVING COUNT(*) > 3
) p ON c.customerNumber = p.customerNumber
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT c.customerNumber, c.customerName, COUNT(p.checkNumber) AS paymentCount FROM customers c JOIN payments p ON c.customerNumber = p.customerNumber GROUP BY c.customerNumber, c.customerName HAVING COUNT(p.checkNumber) > 3;

results.question: give me the total stock value for each vendor
results.generated_sql:
SELECT p.productVendor, SUM(p.quantityInStock * p.buyPrice) AS totalStockValue
FROM products p
GROUP BY p.productVendor
ORDER BY totalStockValue DESC
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT productVendor, SUM(quantityInStock * buyPrice) AS totalStockValue FROM products GROUP BY productVendor ORDER BY totalStockValue DESC;

results.question: what is the second highest payment we got
results.generated_sql:
SELECT amount
FROM payments
ORDER BY amount DESC
LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1
results.ground_truth_sql: SELECT DISTINCT amount FROM payments ORDER BY amount DESC LIMIT 1 OFFSET 1;
